title: File Input/Output in Java
description: Learn file I/O operations by implementing a test report generator that reads test data from files and writes formatted reports. File operations are essential for test automation frameworks that need to read test data, write logs, and generate reports.
instructions: Create a class `FileProcessor` with methods to read from and write to text files
Implement `readTestData(String filename)` that reads test cases from a file and returns an ArrayList of test names
Implement `writeTestReport(String filename, ArrayList<String> tests, ArrayList<String> results)` to write formatted reports
Implement proper exception handling for FileNotFoundException and IOException
Use BufferedReader for efficient file reading and BufferedWriter for writing
Ensure all file streams are properly closed using try-with-resources or finally blocks
learningObjectives: Understand file I/O operations with FileReader and FileWriter
Master BufferedReader and BufferedWriter for efficient file operations
Learn to handle file-related exceptions (IOException, FileNotFoundException)
Practice try-with-resources for automatic resource management
Understand file paths and file system operations

hints.content: Use BufferedReader with FileReader for reading: BufferedReader reader = new BufferedReader(new FileReader(filename)). Read line by line with reader.readLine() in a while loop until it returns null.

For writing, use try-with-resources for automatic closing: try (BufferedWriter writer = new BufferedWriter(new FileWriter(filename))) { writer.write(line); writer.newLine(); }. Catch IOException for error handling.

To append to a file, use FileWriter with append flag: new FileWriter(filename, true). For counting lines, use a counter variable and increment in the while loop: while ((line = reader.readLine()) != null) { count++; }. Always handle FileNotFoundException separately from IOException.
